headline: FLOOR-LIMITED CONFIRMATION (Night 6). Composite 2.2 holds at floor. April 20 Yaccarino + Musk French summons hearing already in prior scan; Amsterdam District Court €100K/day injunction (March 26) still in force; EU Parliament deepfake amendment to AI Act still pending implementation. No NEW acute in-window April 25-28 evidence beyond prior captures. Floor-limitation methodology gap now overdue by 6 nights for this entity specifically.

evidence.finding: 23,000 child-sexualized images in 9 days (Dec 29 2025 - Jan 8 2026); 4.4M total images, 1.8M sexualized depictions of women — per NYT and CCDH.

French criminal probe: complicity in possessing/spreading CSAM, sexually explicit deepfakes, denial of crimes against humanity, organized data-processing manipulation. April 20 Musk+Yaccarino hearing.
UK Information Commissioner's Office investigation ongoing.
EU Parliament voted to ban AI deepfake sexual content (AI Act amendment); Amsterdam District Court €100K/day injunction (€10M cap) still in force.

methodologyNote: FLOOR-LIMITATION METHODOLOGY GAP — SIXTH CONSECUTIVE NIGHT FOR THIS ENTITY. Cumulative evidence stack now includes: CSAM deepfakes (4.4M images in 9 days), French criminal probe (April 20 hearing), Amsterdam injunction, ICO UK probe, EU AI Act deepfake ban, California cease-and-desist, white supremacist content history, antisemitic content, training-consent violations, no transparency reports, conflict-of-interest via Musk's government role. Composite 2.2 cannot meaningfully decrease. Recommend (a) permanent-floor designation framework, (b) cumulative-evidence-tier weighting allowing sub-1 dimension scores, or (c) harm-scale sub-score beyond 0-100. This methodology gap is now overdue by 6+ nights cumulatively across the floor cluster.
